summary.what: Make the post-rewrite hook POSIX so it works under dash
summary.why: The hook used bash-only 'read -d'; under dash (Debian/Ubuntu and CI /bin/sh) it errored 'Illegal option -d' and silently skipped relinking — a latent bug the new hook test exposed on CI, turning it red
summary.how: Replaced 'find -print0 | read -r -d' with newline-delimited 'find | read -r' (safe: entry paths never contain spaces/newlines); verified by forcing the test's sh to dash and reproducing then clearing the failure

notes: Root cause was the local/CI shell split: macOS /bin/sh is bash-in-POSIX-mode (accepts read -d), CI /bin/sh is dash (rejects it), so it passed locally and failed in CI. Reproduced locally by symlinking sh→dash on PATH for the test run. Added a hook comment banning bash-isms to stop the footgun returning. Considered making the test always run under dash-when-available but left it — CI is the POSIX gate and the comment guards the obvious regression.
